title: Living in Boulder blog 8 - 11 - 2017: Plato, Got my new pedals.

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog from Boulder covering his new bike pedals arriving at the shop, plans to start riding regularly outdoors and on rollers, and briefly mentions his weight dropping from 200 to 196 pounds, a possible skin cancer spot the doctor is monitoring, and reading Plato.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: dismissive — riders miss out on a real workout
prioritizes outdoor riding over website/indoor work
acknowledges his schedule is off but expects a ride to tire him out and reset it

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took two days off from making videos because I was catching up on some books.

claims.subject: Play-Toy
claims.claim: I noticed that I didn't have all the Play-Toy installed leather.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got my pedals repaired at the shop and picked them up, so my bike is ready to go.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to clean up my house before going on a ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tested my pedals and they worked, which felt really cool.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I promoted a couple of loans today and then worked on Play-Toy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m going to start riding my bicycle outside and then use rollers while working on the website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ll produce a demo video soon, but my priority is riding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m trying to adjust my schedule to get up earlier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I go on a ride tomorrow, I’ll be tired enough to sleep and get up earlier.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My appetite is low; I had a bowl of cereal and didn’t need to eat all day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I may have skin cancer, but the doctor said it’s not certain yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I weighed 196 pounds, down from 200, after a long ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel firm and energized after riding, unlike people on electric bikes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to adjust my bike’s derailleur and clean it regularly.

claims.subject: Boulder
claims.claim: The recent rain has made everything green like it’s matte.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log for Friday, August 11th, 2017.
Right now it's 12.43 a.m. on August the 12th.
I took two days off of the video because I was catching up on some books.
And I can't remember which ones they were.
But right now, actually, today I was doing Play-Toe.
I noticed I didn't have all the Play-Toe on it.
I don't think I have all of Aristotle either.
So, but it's going to take the backseat today.
I was going to go, because the day before yesterday I got my pedals in the back at the shop.
So I picked them up and my bike's ready to go.
So I was going to go run a ride today.
But I wanted to clean up my house first.
And I wanted to hook up my pedals.
I hadn't made sure that they worked.
And I saw that they worked.
And that shows the watts in the field.
It's really cool.
And I've been high the whole time.
So I feel like I teleported into the future.
It's super exciting.
So, but yeah, then I mowed a couple of loans today.
So then I came back instead of working on Play-Doh and stuff, you know, just trying to tie up.
I was just going to just stop it, all the books I have now.
But because it's Play-Doh, you know, Play-Doh and Aristotle, I have to finish them.
So, but it'll take the back seat.
So, yeah, I'm going to start riding.
Like, front seat goes to riding.
And then the back seat is for the website.
And I'm going to start by doing riding outside.
And then, yeah, and then inside on the rollers, while I'm high, working on the website, I'll do a demo video of that pretty soon.
But I want to do priorities riding.
So, yeah, I'll get up when I get up, you know.
I should try to get to sleep now, but my schedule is a little bit worse.
But, yeah, I need to start getting up in the morning.
But, you know, if I go on a ride tomorrow, I'll be tired enough that I'll be able to get to sleep and then get up earlier.
So, but, yeah, it's interesting.
I don't, my appetite's to, like, nil.
Like, I got up and I had, like, I had, like, a bowl of cereal, two little bowls of cereal.
And then I didn't have to eat all day long, you know, because I'm living off of my blubber.
I thought I had, I think I may have skin cancer here.
The doctor said, and she didn't think it was, so I'm excited.
But I think they're still going to put a biopsy.
But, yeah, so I went in there.
You did it yesterday.
Or it was yesterday.
And, yeah, it was 196 pounds when I was 200 before.
So I lost four pounds in four rides.
So, like I said, I was in a pound of a ride.
You know, that's a lot of calories.
It saves money on the food, too.
But it's cool because when I come back from a ride, I feel my stomach.
And I can feel, like, firm, firmer and firmer.
It's really cool.
I pity those people on the electric bikes because they're just not getting a workout now.
So, there's so many different kinds of bikes.
I want one of each.
So, yeah, so that's what's going on.
I need to make a playlist, but it's getting kind of light.
And I also need to adjust my bike.
Like, my derailers are screwed up and it's dirty.
I need to get it in the habit of cleaning it every time because it's just so new and shiny.
And it picks up a lot of, like, rocks sticking to it from all the rain.
And this has been the rainiest summer here I've ever seen.
So, everything's green like it's made.
I think I'm talking too much.
Let's dock this.
Okay.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
